signals.name: Forced Liquidation
signals.text: Producers selling into weakness — suggests stock overhang or cash flow pressure regardless of price.

signals.name: Forced Liquidation
signals.text: Producers selling into weakness — suggests stock overhang or cash flow pressure regardless of price.

signals.name: Producer Exhaustion
signals.text: Producers near fully hedged (>75th pct, 52-week) — limited additional selling capacity from this actor.

signals.name: Normal Coverage
signals.text: Roasters adding coverage into price weakness — standard buying flow.

signals.name: Roaster Dry Powder
signals.text: Roasters near fully covered (>75th pct) — limited additional buying capacity from this actor.

signals.name: Normal Coverage
signals.text: Roasters adding coverage into price weakness — standard buying flow.

signals.name: Fund Long Liquidation
signals.text: Funds reducing longs into falling price — bearish momentum, trend following.

signals.name: Contrarian Fund Buying
signals.text: Funds buying into weakness — contrarian positioning. Cross-reference OI sequence and the brother contract.

signals.name: Fund Long Overhang
signals.text: Large speculative long position (>75th pct) with price already reversing — liquidation underway, momentum risk elevated.

signals.name: Fund Bearish Exhaustion
signals.text: Funds adding shorts near capacity (>75th pct) — bearish momentum likely limited, short covering risk increasing.

signals.name: Reluctant Short Cover
signals.text: Funds covering shorts despite falling price — may signal exhaustion of bearish thesis.

signals.name: Speculative Conviction Bearish
signals.text: Funds reducing longs and adding shorts simultaneously — strong bearish conviction.

signals.name: Net Short Exhaustion
signals.text: MM net position near 52-week low (<25th pct) — speculative net shorts at extreme, limited room to extend. Short-covering catalyst risk elevated.

signals.name: Speculative Conviction Bullish
signals.text: Funds adding longs and covering shorts simultaneously — strong bullish conviction.

signals.name: Net Long Exhaustion
signals.text: MM net long position near 52-week high (>75th pct) — speculative net longs at extreme, limited room to add. Mean-reversion risk elevated.

signals.name: Bearish Capitulation
signals.text: Funds liquidating longs while producers add hedges — broad bearish alignment. Roaster behavior confirms breadth: if they're also reducing coverage, conviction is across all actors.

signals.name: Forced Market
signals.text: Funds buying against producer selling into falling price — min/max levels of both actors and daily OI sequence reveal the dominant pressure.

signals.name: Roaster Coverage Opportunity
signals.text: Funds reducing while roasters opportunistically add coverage into weakness — puts a floor under the price decline.

signals.name: Bullish Concentration
signals.text: MM net position growing bullish while net trader count shrinks — the bullish move is driven by fewer actors. Concentration increases reversal fragility.

signals.name: Backwardation Incentive
signals.text: KC curve in backwardation — front premium rewards long holders, supportive of bullish positioning. Verify that roll yield exceeds the risk-free rate to confirm the incentive is real.

signals.name: Inversion Easing
signals.text: KC backwardation losing strength — reduces incentive for longs to hold, may trigger gradual long liquidation. Check if easing coincides with roll window.

signals.name: Backwardation Incentive
signals.text: RC curve in backwardation — front premium rewards long holders, supportive of bullish positioning. Verify that roll yield exceeds the risk-free rate to confirm the incentive is real.

signals.name: Inversion Easing
signals.text: RC backwardation losing strength — reduces incentive for longs to hold, may trigger gradual long liquidation. Check if easing coincides with roll window.

signals.name: Spreading Increase
signals.text: Spreading OI increasing without directional price move — funds positioning across the curve. Often precedes a decisive directional move; the calendar-spread direction hints which way.

signals.name: Flowering Disruption / Blossom Drop
signals.text: colombia/Caldas: Potential false flowering or dropped blossoms. Harvest delay.  [spei_3=-2.17, forecast_7d_rain=182.1]

signals.name: Flowering Disruption / Blossom Drop
signals.text: vn/Lam Dong: Potential false flowering or dropped blossoms. Harvest delay.  [spei_3=-1.17, forecast_7d_rain=74.3]

signals.name: Defensive De-hedging
signals.text: Producers covering shorts on weakness — normal deleveraging, low conviction.

signals.name: Normal Coverage
signals.text: Roasters adding coverage into price weakness — standard buying flow.

signals.name: Coverage Reduction
signals.text: Roasters reducing coverage on weakness — expecting further downside or reducing exposure.

signals.name: Commercial Convergence Bullish
signals.text: Both commercials aligned bullish — strong fundamental demand signal.

signals.text: Funds reducing longs into falling price — bearish momentum, trend following.

signals.name: Contrarian Fund Buying

signals.name: Fund Bearish Entry
signals.text: Funds building shorts into price weakness — conviction depends on magnitude of the move.

signals.name: Fund Bearish Exhaustion
signals.text: Funds adding shorts near capacity (>75th pct) — bearish momentum likely limited, short covering risk increasing.

signals.name: Short Concentration
signals.text: MM short OI rising while number of short traders falls — fewer, larger short positions. Concentration increases squeeze fragility: a stop-out by one large actor can compress the market rapidly.

signals.name: Speculative Conviction Bearish
signals.text: Funds reducing longs and adding shorts simultaneously — strong bearish conviction.

signals.name: Speculative Confusion
signals.text: Both sides growing — net position will decide which side dominates.

signals.name: Net Short Exhaustion
signals.text: MM net position near 52-week low (<25th pct) — speculative net shorts at extreme, limited room to extend. Short-covering catalyst risk elevated.

signals.name: Roaster Coverage Opportunity
signals.text: Funds reducing while roasters opportunistically add coverage into weakness — puts a floor under the price decline.

signals.name: Backwardation Incentive

signals.name: Backwardation Incentive
signals.text: RC curve in backwardation — front premium rewards long holders, supportive of bullish positioning. Verify that roll yield exceeds the risk-free rate to confirm the incentive is real.

signals.name: Oversold Opportunity
signals.text: KC technically oversold (≤25th pct) with funds near minimum exposure — high potential for mean-reversion rally. If contango is deep, re-entry incentive for funds is reduced. Watch for catalyst.

signals.name: Spreading Decrease
signals.text: Funds collapsing spread positions — may signal transition to outright directional positioning. Watch which direction longs and shorts move next.

signals.name: Bullish De-hedging
signals.text: Producers lifting hedges into rising price — signals expectation of further upside.

signals.name: Normal Hedging
signals.text: Producers locking in levels into price strength — standard hedging flow.

signals.name: Unusual Liquidation
signals.text: Roasters reducing coverage into rising price — may signal demand destruction or blend substitution toward Robusta.

signals.name: Fund Bullish Entry
signals.text: Funds building longs into price strength — conviction depends on magnitude of the move.

signals.name: Fund Bullish Entry
signals.text: Funds building longs into price strength — conviction depends on magnitude of the move.

signals.name: Long Concentration
signals.text: MM long OI rising while number of long traders falls — fewer, larger positions. Concentration increases fragility: a forced exit by one actor can cascade.

signals.name: Contrarian Fund Shorting
signals.text: Funds shorting into rising price — contrarian positioning, betting on reversal.

signals.name: Short Concentration
signals.text: MM short OI rising while number of short traders falls — fewer, larger short positions. Concentration increases squeeze fragility: a stop-out by one large actor can compress the market rapidly.

signals.name: Contrarian Fund Shorting
signals.text: Funds shorting into rising price — contrarian positioning, betting on reversal.

signals.name: Fund Short Squeeze Risk
signals.text: Large speculative short position (>75th pct) with price already rising — short squeeze underway, covering pressure accelerating.

signals.text: Both sides growing — net position will decide which side dominates.

signals.name: Net Short Exhaustion

signals.name: Squeeze Setup
signals.text: Funds and producers both bullish simultaneously — strong squeeze risk, limited natural selling. Confirm with roaster coverage levels: if under-covered, squeeze is amplified.

signals.name: Classic Bullish Flow
signals.text: Funds buying against producer hedging into rising price — textbook bullish market structure. Producers locking in levels, funds betting on further upside.

signals.name: Contrarian Divergence
signals.text: Funds adding longs while roasters reduce coverage into rising price — the brother contract (LDN, shown opposite) reveals whether blend switching is at play. Substitution would undermine the bullish thesis.

signals.name: Fund Buying vs Roaster Absence
signals.text: Funds driving price higher but roasters not participating — rally sustainable only if roasters are eventually forced to cover. Roaster coverage percentile and calendar spread carry costs gate the squeeze.

signals.name: Backwardation Incentive
signals.text: KC curve in backwardation — front premium rewards long holders, supportive of bullish positioning. Verify that roll yield exceeds the risk-free rate to confirm the incentive is real.

signals.name: Backwardation Incentive
signals.text: RC curve in backwardation — front premium rewards long holders, supportive of bullish positioning. Verify that roll yield exceeds the risk-free rate to confirm the incentive is real.

signals.name: Oversold Divergence
signals.text: KC technically oversold but price already recovering — short covering likely driving the move. Sustainable only if commercial buyers confirm with increased coverage.

signals.text: Spreading OI increasing while backwardation deepens — funds likely harvesting backwardation premium. Not a directional signal but confirms curve structure is attracting capital.

signals.name: Contrarian Divergence
signals.text: Funds adding longs while roasters reduce coverage into rising price — the brother contract (NY, shown opposite) reveals whether blend switching is at play. Substitution would undermine the bullish thesis.

signals.name: Backwardation Incentive
signals.text: KC curve in backwardation — front premium rewards long holders, supportive of bullish positioning. Verify that roll yield exceeds the risk-free rate to confirm the incentive is real.

signals.name: Inversion Easing
signals.text: KC backwardation losing strength — reduces incentive for longs to hold, may trigger gradual long liquidation. Check if easing coincides with roll window.

signals.name: Backwardation Incentive
signals.text: RC curve in backwardation — front premium rewards long holders, supportive of bullish positioning. Verify that roll yield exceeds the risk-free rate to confirm the incentive is real.

signals.name: Inversion Easing
signals.text: RC backwardation losing strength — reduces incentive for longs to hold, may trigger gradual long liquidation. Check if easing coincides with roll window.

signals.name: Oversold Divergence
signals.text: KC technically oversold but price already recovering — short covering likely driving the move. Sustainable only if commercial buyers confirm with increased coverage.

signals.name: Oversold Divergence
signals.text: RC technically oversold but price already recovering — short covering likely driving the move. Sustainable only if commercial buyers confirm with increased coverage.

signals.name: Forced Liquidation
signals.text: Producers selling into weakness — suggests stock overhang or cash flow pressure regardless of price.

signals.name: Forced Liquidation
signals.text: Producers selling into weakness — suggests stock overhang or cash flow pressure regardless of price.

signals.name: Producer Exhaustion
signals.text: Producers near fully hedged (>75th pct, 52-week) — limited additional selling capacity from this actor.

signals.name: Normal Coverage
signals.text: Roasters adding coverage into price weakness — standard buying flow.

signals.name: Roaster Dry Powder
signals.text: Roasters near fully covered (>75th pct) — limited additional buying capacity from this actor.

signals.name: Normal Coverage
signals.text: Roasters adding coverage into price weakness — standard buying flow.

signals.name: Fund Long Liquidation
signals.text: Funds reducing longs into falling price — bearish momentum, trend following.

signals.name: Contrarian Fund Buying
signals.text: Funds buying into weakness — contrarian positioning. Cross-reference OI sequence and the brother contract.

signals.name: Fund Long Overhang
signals.text: Large speculative long position (>75th pct) with price already reversing — liquidation underway, momentum risk elevated.

signals.name: Fund Bearish Exhaustion
signals.text: Funds adding shorts near capacity (>75th pct) — bearish momentum likely limited, short covering risk increasing.

signals.name: Reluctant Short Cover
signals.text: Funds covering shorts despite falling price — may signal exhaustion of bearish thesis.

signals.name: Speculative Conviction Bearish
signals.text: Funds reducing longs and adding shorts simultaneously — strong bearish conviction.

signals.name: Net Short Exhaustion
signals.text: MM net position near 52-week low (<25th pct) — speculative net shorts at extreme, limited room to extend. Short-covering catalyst risk elevated.

signals.name: Speculative Conviction Bullish
signals.text: Funds adding longs and covering shorts simultaneously — strong bullish conviction.

signals.name: Net Long Exhaustion
signals.text: MM net long position near 52-week high (>75th pct) — speculative net longs at extreme, limited room to add. Mean-reversion risk elevated.

signals.name: Bearish Capitulation
signals.text: Funds liquidating longs while producers add hedges — broad bearish alignment. Roaster behavior confirms breadth: if they're also reducing coverage, conviction is across all actors.

signals.name: Forced Market
signals.text: Funds buying against producer selling into falling price — min/max levels of both actors and daily OI sequence reveal the dominant pressure.

signals.name: Roaster Coverage Opportunity
signals.text: Funds reducing while roasters opportunistically add coverage into weakness — puts a floor under the price decline.

signals.name: Bullish Concentration
signals.text: MM net position growing bullish while net trader count shrinks — the bullish move is driven by fewer actors. Concentration increases reversal fragility.

signals.name: Backwardation Incentive
signals.text: KC curve in backwardation — front premium rewards long holders, supportive of bullish positioning. Verify that roll yield exceeds the risk-free rate to confirm the incentive is real.

signals.name: Inversion Easing
signals.text: KC backwardation losing strength — reduces incentive for longs to hold, may trigger gradual long liquidation. Check if easing coincides with roll window.

signals.name: Backwardation Incentive
signals.text: RC curve in backwardation — front premium rewards long holders, supportive of bullish positioning. Verify that roll yield exceeds the risk-free rate to confirm the incentive is real.

signals.name: Inversion Easing
signals.text: RC backwardation losing strength — reduces incentive for longs to hold, may trigger gradual long liquidation. Check if easing coincides with roll window.

signals.name: Spreading Increase
signals.text: Spreading OI increasing without directional price move — funds positioning across the curve. Often precedes a decisive directional move; the calendar-spread direction hints which way.
